summary: Decompiled the previously empty sysdolphin/baselib implementation for hsd_803A949C and exposed its prototype in hsd_3A94.h. The function is a large CARD-related command completion dispatcher using local reconstructed card state/command/block structs, close/retry handling, checksum/validation helpers, and per-command memcpy/memcmp paths; decomp.dev reported hsd_803A949C improved by +2852 bytes from 0.00% to 58.78%.

key_files.role: Added 491 lines implementing hsd_803A949C plus file-local structs CardStateExt, CardCmdEntry, CardBlock and helper functions.
Added the public prototype /* 3A949C */ void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1);

what_changed: Filled in hsd_3A94.c from an effectively empty source file with includes for hsd_3A94.h, hsd_3AA7.h, hsd_3B2B.h, hsd_3B2E.h, dolphin/card.h, and string.h.
Declared extern globals hsd_804D7980, hsd_804D7988, and hsd_804D799C used as command queue/status state.
Added local reconstructed structs CardStateExt, CardCmdEntry, and CardBlock with explicit offset comments and padding fields.
Added static helpers hsd_803A949C_GetEntry, hsd_803A949C_Close, hsd_803A949C_IconSize, and hsd_803A949C_FileId.
Implemented hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) as a switch on entry->x0 with handled command cases 1, 2, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 15, and 16.
Implemented CARD close handling through CARDClose retries, propagating arg1 and negative close results into hsd_804D7988.
Added data verification/copy paths using hsd_803B31CC, hsd_803B2B20, hsd_803AC558, memcpy, and memcmp across card header, icon, data, and checksum-like trailer regions.
Completed the dispatcher by clearing entry->x0, clearing hsd_804D799C, and advancing hsd_804D7980 modulo 128.
Added the hsd_803A949C prototype to hsd_3A94.h.
smart_moves: Kept uncertain layouts local to hsd_3A94.c, avoiding premature public exposure of CardStateExt/CardCmdEntry/CardBlock while still documenting offsets.
Factored repeated low-level behavior into small static helpers: current queue entry lookup, CARDClose retry loop, icon-size selection, and two-byte file-id extraction.
Preserved opaque numeric command cases and error/sentinel values such as -0x105, -0x107, 2, and -0x7FFF rather than inventing unsupported semantic names.
Separated first-sector and later-sector handling with explicit icon_size, hdr_plus_icon, remaining, data_offset, and sector_size temporaries, making the large dispatcher easier to audit.
Used existing Dolphin and baselib primitives rather than reimplementing them: CARDFileInfo/CARDClose for file state, hsd_803B31CC for validation, and hsd_803B2B20 for computed trailer/check data.
decomp_lessons: For large asynchronous CARD-style callbacks, reconstruct the outer dispatcher first by command opcode, then pull out tiny repeated idioms into file-local helpers to keep control flow matchable.
Offset-commented local structs are useful when field semantics are incomplete: they allow exact accesses and padding without forcing unstable names into headers.
Header changes can stay minimal: this PR only exported the newly implemented function while keeping helper functions and provisional struct definitions private.
When evidence for names is weak, retain address-based function names and x-offset fields; add descriptive helper suffixes only where the operation is directly visible from code.
Card save/load routines often split header, icon, payload, and trailer/check regions; matching work should preserve those boundary calculations exactly.
naming_conventions: File-local helpers use the address prefix plus descriptive suffixes: hsd_803A949C_GetEntry, hsd_803A949C_Close, hsd_803A949C_IconSize, hsd_803A949C_FileId.
Unknown fields use x-offset names such as x0, x8, x24, x170, x270, x370, x3B0, x430, and x460.
Padding fields use pad_OFFSET names such as pad_04, pad_28, pad_3B1, pad_00, and pad_13.
Local domain structs use readable Card-prefixed typedefs even while their fields remain mostly unknown: CardStateExt, CardCmdEntry, CardBlock.
Data globals remain address-named: hsd_804D7980, hsd_804D7988, hsd_804D799C, and hsd_804D1148.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a do-while CARDClose retry loop that stops when CARDClose no longer returns -1 or after 10 attempts.
Indexed the command queue as &((CardCmdEntry*) hsd_804D1148)[hsd_804D7980] and advanced hsd_804D7980 with modulo 128 at function exit.
Preserved command dispatch as a numeric switch over entry->x0 instead of abstracting to enums before the opcode meanings are proven.
Computed wrapped block offsets with (state->x24 + 0x30) % state->x8 for slot-zero/special block handling.

Used exact region math for card contents: icon_size from state->x3B0, hdr_plus_icon = icon_size + 0x40, remaining = state->x24 - sector_size * entry->x8, and data_offset = sector_size * entry->x8 - 0x40 - icon_size.
Kept casts visible where they likely affect generated code and pointer arithmetic, including (void*) entry fields, (u8*) entry->x10 + data_offset, and (u32) remaining > sector_size.
review_feedback: No human PR body or review comments were captured in the slice.
decomp-dev bot reported one unmatched-item improvement for GALE01: main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94 item hsd_803A949C improved by +2852 bytes, from 0.00% to 58.78%.
follow_up_queries: Find writers of hsd_804D1148/CardCmdEntry to map entry->x0 command values 1, 2, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 15, and 16 to semantic operation names.
Identify the true layout and ownership of CardStateExt, especially x0 buffer, x8 sector size, x24 size/offset, x170/x270 arrays, x370 header, x3B0 icon mode, x430 trailer/check data, and x460 bounds.
Search for hsd_803B31CC and hsd_803B2B20 call sites to confirm whether they are checksum, validation, or digest helpers.
Check whether CardCmdEntry and CardStateExt should eventually move from hsd_3A94.c into a shared private header once more users are decompiled.
Investigate callers of hsd_803A949C to determine whether arg1 is a CARD async result/status callback parameter.
